questions.question: What is the correct abbreviated form of the IPv6 address 2001:0DB8:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0001?
questions.explanation: Apply both abbreviation rules: first remove leading zeros (0DB8 becomes DB8, 0001 becomes 1), then replace consecutive groups of all zeros with :: (used only once). The most abbreviated form is 2001:DB8::1.

questions.question: A host needs to automatically obtain an IPv6 address without any DHCP server. Which process does it use?
options: ARP request to the default gateway
SLAAC with Router Solicitation and Router Advertisement
questions.explanation: SLAAC (Stateless Address Autoconfiguration) allows a host to build its own IPv6 address by combining the prefix received in a Router Advertisement (RA) with a self-generated interface ID (via EUI-64 or random). No DHCP server is required.

questions.question: Which NDP message type replaces the function of an ARP request in IPv6?
questions.explanation: Neighbor Solicitation (NS), ICMPv6 type 135, is used to resolve an IPv6 address to a MAC address, directly replacing the ARP request used in IPv4. The target responds with a Neighbor Advertisement (NA).

questions.question: Given the MAC address 00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E, what is the EUI-64 generated interface ID?
questions.explanation: EUI-64 splits the MAC in half, inserts FF:FE in the middle (00:1A:2B:FF:FE:3C:4D:5E), then flips the 7th bit of the first byte. The first byte 00 (00000000) becomes 02 (00000010), giving 021A:2BFF:FE3C:4D5E.

questions.question: Which IPv6 address type is identified by the prefix FE80::/10 and is required on every IPv6-enabled interface?
questions.explanation: Link-local addresses use the prefix FE80::/10 and are automatically created on every IPv6-enabled interface. They are not routable beyond the local link and are essential for NDP, OSPFv3 neighbor relationships, and next-hop routing.
